name: Páramo del Arroyo — circular from Burgos
name_original: Páramo del Arroyo — circular desde Burgos
region_intro: Touring cycling around Arlanzon offers routes through diverse landscapes in the Sierra de la Demanda mountains, east of Burgos. The region features varied terrain, including mountainous areas, forests, and geological formations. The Arlanzón River flows through the area, providing riverside environments, while the Arlanzón Reservoir offers mountain views and picturesque scenery. Dedicated greenways, such as the Vía Verde de la Sierra de la Demanda, provide marked paths suitable for cycling. Best touring cycling routes around Arlanzon The most popular touring cycling route is Los Ausines from Burgos — circular via the Santander-Mediterranean Greenway and the El Cid Cycle Route, a 27.8 miles (44.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 45 minutes to complete. This moderate route utilizes sections of the Santander-Mediterranean Greenway. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Burgos: El Cid and Arlanzón River Loop, an easy 15.0 miles (24.2 km) path. This route follows the Arlanzón River, offering a natural and accessible experience. Local touring cyclists also love the Rioseras from Burgos — circular via the Santander-Mediterranean Greenway, a 25.4 miles (40.8 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 34 minutes. Touring cycling around Arlanzon is defined by mountainous terrain, tranquil river valleys, and scenic reservoir views. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside paths to more challenging routes through the Sierra de la Demanda. The routes in Arlanzon are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from around 490 reviews. More than 4000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Arlanzon's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Arlanzon?
A: There are over 280 touring cycling routes around Arlanzon, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when touring cycling around Arlanzon?
A: The terrain around Arlanzon is quite varied. You can expect mountainous areas within the Sierra de la Demanda, tranquil river valleys along the Arlanzón River, and scenic paths around the Arlanzón Reservoir. Greenways like the Vía Verde de la Sierra de la Demanda offer smoother, dedicated cycling paths.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly touring cycling routes in Arlanzon?
A: Yes, Arlanzon offers over 50 easy touring cycling routes. A great option for families or beginners is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1030131669/burgos-el-cid-and-arlanzon-river-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Burgos: El Cid and Arlanzón River Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which follows the river and provides an accessible experience. The Arlanzón River Trail is also known for being easy and suitable for all fitness levels.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I can see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer views of the Sierra de la Demanda mountains, the Arlanzón River, and the picturesque Arlanzón Reservoir. You might also encounter historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2729971" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Atapuerca Archaeological Site&lt;/a&gt;, a UNESCO World Heritage site, which is near the reservoir area. The &lt;a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Arlanz%C3%B3n_(river)" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arlanzón River&lt;/a&gt; itself is home to various bird species, especially near Fuentes Blancas Park.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Arlanzon?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in the Arlanzon area are circular. Examples include the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027721127/los-ausines-from-burgos-circular-via-the-santander-mediterranean-greenway-and-the-el-cid-cycle-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Ausines from Burgos — circular via the Santander-Mediterranean Greenway and the El Cid Cycle Route&lt;/a&gt; and the easy &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1030321837/castrillo-del-val-from-burgos-circular-by-san-medel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castrillo del Val from Burgos — circular by San Medel&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Arlanzon?
A: The region experiences varied weather. While specific recommendations depend on personal preference, spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures for cycling. Summers can be warm, and winters, especially in the Sierra de la Demanda, can be cold and snowy, making some routes more challenging.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about the routes in Arlanzon?
A: The touring cycling routes around Arlanzon are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from nearly 500 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, from mountainous terrain to tranquil river valleys, and the scenic views, particularly around the Arlanzón Reservoir.

Q: Are there any routes that follow old railway lines or greenways?
A: Absolutely. A significant feature for touring cyclists is the Vía Verde de la Sierra de la Demanda, which follows an old mining train route. This greenway offers a marked, scenic path through wooded areas and alongside the Arlanzón Reservoir, providing a unique cycling experience.

Q: Can I find routes that are more challenging for advanced touring cyclists?
A: Yes, Arlanzon offers over 110 difficult touring cycling routes for those seeking a greater challenge. These routes often venture into the more mountainous parts of the Sierra de la Demanda. An example of a more demanding route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027983317/poza-de-la-sal-from-sotopalacios-gravel-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Poza de la Sal from Sotopalacios — gravel circular&lt;/a&gt;, which features significant elevation changes.

Q: Are there any specific routes that offer views of the Arlanzón Reservoir?
A: Yes, the road bordering the Arlanzón Reservoir offers incredible mountain views, with clouds often reflecting in the water. The GR 82 trail also traces the reservoir, providing continuous scenic views of the marsh and its surrounding landscape. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027978880/rioseras-from-burgos-circular-via-the-santander-mediterranean-greenway" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rioseras from Burgos — circular via the Santander-Mediterranean Greenway&lt;/a&gt; may also offer glimpses of this beautiful feature.

Q: Where can I find information about parking for bike trails near Arlanzon?
A: While specific parking details for each trailhead are best checked on individual route pages, many routes originating from Burgos or nearby villages will have public parking options. For routes along the Arlanzón River, areas like Fuentes Blancas Park in Burgos typically offer parking facilities.
tips:
Architecture friends and those interested in history are in the right place in Burgos: from the narrow old town streets, to the spacious Plaza Mayor, the impressive cathedral, various venerable monasteries and the striking castle ruins above the city, there is an incredible amount to discover here.

The Cathedral of Burgos with its many ornaments is of course a particularly impressive building that you definitely have to see. But a visit from the inside is also worthwhile, because, among other things, you can marvel at the final resting place of the Castilian knight and Spanish national hero El Cid and his wife Doña Jimena. The bones were moved here from the Monastery of San Pedro de Cardeña in 1835 to create a more dignified tomb for the hero. His legendary sword "Tizona" can also be seen here.

The Solar del Cid is a monument built in the 18th century in the place where it was thought, without historical evidence, that the house of the Cid was located. It consists of three monoliths with different coats of arms and a plaque commemorating the link between Cid and Burgos: "Rodrigo Díaz de Vivar, called Cid Campeador, had his home on this site and was born in 1026. He died in Valencia in 1099 and was transferred his body to the monastery of San Pedro de Cardeña, near this city, which to perpetuate the memory of such an enlightened plot of his son and hero from Burgos, erected this monument on the ancient ruins in 1784, when Carlos III reigned."

Fascinating 18th century church in the town of Páramo del Arroyo, with a classicist baroque altarpiece consisting of three streets, and two bodies and an attic. From the vicinity of the church you can see the wind farm "Páramo".

waypoints.name: Church of San Vicente Mártir of Páramo del Arroyo
